prompt: Order intake is down hard. Every POST — including orders that validated fine last week — now 500s, and the crash log shows the handler goroutine dying with `runtime error: invalid memory address or nil pointer dereference` with `(*APIError).Error` at the top of the stack, called from Build. Repro is trivial: Handle any well-formed order (id set, positive qty, at least one item) and it panics before the store layer ever sees the order. Separately, ops noticed that when the warehouse store rejects an order, their alerting can no longer classify the failure — errors.As comes back empty-handed where it used to find our structured error with the code intact. Rejected-order envelopes and their JSON shape still look right. Fix envelope.go so valid orders flow through to the store and come back as accepted envelopes, and prove it with `go test -race -timeout 30s ./...`. envelope_test.go is the contract — don't touch it.

runtime_debug.baseline_evidence: build_cmd exits 0 (ok, no tests to run). Repro panics: SIGSEGV nil pointer dereference in (*APIError).Error called from Build/Handle on a fully valid order; TestProcessReturnsNilForValidOrder also fails because Process returns a non-nil error interface, and TestStoreFailureKeepsIdentity fails errors.As classification.
